lecture.title: 137. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 9-11 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.11.slok:
अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः |
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ||१२-११||
12.11.transliteration:
athaitadapyaśakto.asi kartuṃ madyogamāśritaḥ .
sarvakarmaphalatyāgaṃ tataḥ kuru yatātmavān ||12-11||

translation.text: If thou art unable to do even this, then taking refuge in Me, abandon the fruit of all action, self-controlled.

lecture.title: 137. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 9-11 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: Better indeed is knowledge than (blind) Abhyâsa; meditation (with knowledge) is more esteemed than (mere) knowledge; than meditation the renunciation of the fruit of action; peace immediately follows renunciation.

lecture.title: 138. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 12-13 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.13.slok:
अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च |
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ||१२-१३||
12.13.transliteration:
adveṣṭā sarvabhūtānāṃ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca .
nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ samaduḥkhasukhaḥ kṣamī ||12-13||

translation.text: He who hates no creature, and is friendly and compassionate towards all, who is free from the feelings of 'I and mine,' even-minded in pain and pleasure, forbearing, ever content, steady in meditation, self-controlled, and possessed of firm conviction, with mind and intellect fixed on Me,—he who is thus devoted to Me, is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 139. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 13-14 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.14.slok:
सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः |
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ||१२-१४||
12.14.transliteration:
santuṣṭaḥ satataṃ yogī yatātmā dṛḍhaniścayaḥ .
mayyarpitamanobuddhiryo madbhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||12-14||

translation.text: He who hates no creature, and is friendly and compassionate towards all, who is free from the feelings of 'I and mine,' even-minded in pain and pleasure, forbearing, ever content, steady in meditation, self-controlled, and possessed of firm conviction, with mind and intellect fixed on Me,—he who is thus devoted to Me, is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 139. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 13-14 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.15.slok:
यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः |
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ||१२-१५||
12.15.transliteration:
yasmānnodvijate loko lokānnodvijate ca yaḥ .
harṣāmarṣabhayodvegairmukto yaḥ sa ca me priyaḥ ||12-15||

translation.text: He by whom the world is not agitated and who cannot be agitated by the world, who is freed from joy, envy, fear and anxiety,—he is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 140. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verse 15 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.16.slok:
अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः |
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ||१२-१६||
12.16.transliteration:
anapekṣaḥ śucirdakṣa udāsīno gatavyathaḥ .
sarvārambhaparityāgī yo madbhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||12-16||

translation.text: He who is free from dependence, who is pure, prompt, unconcerned, untroubled, renouncing every undertaking,—he who is thus devoted to Me, is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 141. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 16-19 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.17.slok:
यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति |
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ||१२-१७||
12.17.transliteration:
yo na hṛṣyati na dveṣṭi na śocati na kāṅkṣati .
śubhāśubhaparityāgī bhaktimānyaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||12-17||

translation.text: He who neither rejoices, nor hates, nor grieves, nor desires, renouncing good and evil, full of devotion, he is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 141. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 16-19 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.18.slok:
समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः |
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ||१२-१८||
12.18.transliteration:
samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca tathā mānāpamānayoḥ .
śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkheṣu samaḥ saṅgavivarjitaḥ ||12-18||

translation.text: He who is the same to friend and foe, and also in honour and dishonour; who is the same in heat and cold, and in pleasure and pain; who is free from attachment; to whom censure and praise are equal; who is silent, content with anything, homeless, steady-minded, full of devotion,—that man is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 142. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 18-20 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.19.slok:
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् |
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ||१२-१९||
12.19.transliteration:
tulyanindāstutirmaunī santuṣṭo yena kenacit .
aniketaḥ sthiramatirbhaktimānme priyo naraḥ ||12-19||

translation.text: He who is the same to friend and foe, and also in honour and dishonour; who is the same in heat and cold, and in pleasure and pain; who is free from attachment; to whom censure and praise are equal; who is silent, content with anything, homeless, steady-minded, full of devotion,—that man is dear to Me.

lecture.title: 142. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 18-20 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

12.20.slok:
ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते |
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ||१२-२०||
12.20.transliteration:
ye tu dharmyāmṛtamidaṃ yathoktaṃ paryupāsate .
śraddadhānā matparamā bhaktāste.atīva me priyāḥ ||12-20||

translation.text: And they who follow this Immortal Dharma, as described above, endued with Shraddhâ, regarding Me as the Supreme Goal, and devoted,—they are exceedingly dear to Me.

lecture.title: 142. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 12 Verses 18-20 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.1.slok:
अर्जुन उवाच |
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च |
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ||१३-१||
13.1.transliteration:
arjuna uvāca .
prakṛtiṃ puruṣaṃ caiva kṣetraṃ kṣetrajñameva ca .
etadveditumicchāmi jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ ca keśava ||13-1||

translation.text: This body, O son of Kunti, is called Kshetra, and he who knows it is called Kshetrajna by those who know of them (Kshetra and Kshetrajna).
translation.speaker: The Blessed Lord said:

lecture.title: 143. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 13, Verses 1-2 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.2.slok:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते |
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ||१३-२||
13.2.transliteration:
śrībhagavānuvāca .
idaṃ śarīraṃ kaunteya kṣetramityabhidhīyate .
etadyo vetti taṃ prāhuḥ kṣetrajña iti tadvidaḥ ||13-2||

translation.text: Me do thou also know, O descendant of Bharata, to be Kshetrajna in all Kshetras. The knowledge of Kshetra and Kshetrajna is considered by Me to be the knowledge.

lecture.title: 146. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verse 2 (Cont'd) I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.3.slok:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत |
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ||१३-३||
13.3.transliteration:
kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata .
kṣetrakṣetrajñayorjñānaṃ yattajjñānaṃ mataṃ mama ||13-3||

translation.text: What the Kshetra is, what its properties are, what are its modifications, what effects arise from what causes, and also who He is and what His powers are, that hear from Me in brief.

lecture.title: 147. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 13, Verses 3-6 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.4.slok:
तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् |
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु ||१३-४||
13.4.transliteration:
tatkṣetraṃ yacca yādṛkca yadvikāri yataśca yat .
sa ca yo yatprabhāvaśca tatsamāsena me śṛṇu ||13-4||

translation.text: (This truth) has been sung by Rishis in many ways, in various distinctive chants, in passages indicative of Brahman, full of reasoning, and convincing.

lecture.title: 147. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 13, Verses 3-6 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.5.slok:
ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् |
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः ||१३-५||
13.5.transliteration:
ṛṣibhirbahudhā gītaṃ chandobhirvividhaiḥ pṛthak .
brahmasūtrapadaiścaiva hetumadbhirviniścitaiḥ ||13-5||

translation.text: The great Elements, Egoism, Intellect, as also the Unmanifested (Mulâ Prakriti), the ten senses and the one (mind), and the five objects of the senses; desire, hatred, pleasure, pain, the, aggregate, intelligence, fortitude,—the Kshetra has been thus briefly described with its modifications.

lecture.title: 147. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 13, Verses 3-6 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.6.slok:
महाभूतान्यहंकारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च |
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ||१३-६||
13.6.transliteration:
mahābhūtānyahaṃkāro buddhiravyaktameva ca .
indriyāṇi daśaikaṃ ca pañca cendriyagocarāḥ ||13-6||

translation.text: The great Elements, Egoism, Intellect, as also the Unmanifested (Mulâ Prakriti), the ten senses and the one (mind), and the five objects of the senses; desire, hatred, pleasure, pain, the, aggregate, intelligence, fortitude,—the Kshetra has been thus briefly described with its modifications.

lecture.title: 147. Bhagavad Gita I Chapter 13, Verses 3-6 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.7.slok:
इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं संघातश्चेतना धृतिः |
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ||१३-७||
13.7.transliteration:
icchā dveṣaḥ sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ saṃghātaścetanā dhṛtiḥ .
etatkṣetraṃ samāsena savikāramudāhṛtam ||13-7||

translation.text: Humility, unpretentiousness, non-injury, forbearance, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadiness, self-control;

lecture.title: 150. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verse 7 Cont'd I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: The renunciation of sense-objects, and also absence of egoism; reflection on the evils of birth, death, old age, sickness and pain;

lecture.title: 151. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 13, Verses 8-9 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: Non-attachment, non-identification of self with son, wife, home, and the rest, and constant even-mindedness in the occurrence of the desirable and the un-undesirable;

lecture.title: 151. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 13, Verses 8-9 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: Unswerving devotion to Me by the Yoga of non-separation, resort to sequestered places, distaste for the society of men;

lecture.title: 152. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verse 10 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: Constant application to spiritual knowledge, understanding of the end of true knowledge: this is declared to be knowledge, and what is opposed to it is ignorance.

lecture.title: 153. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 11-12 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: I shall describe that which has to be known, knowing which one attains to immortality, the beginningless Supreme Brahman. It is called neither being nor non-being.

lecture.title: 153. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 11-12 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.13.slok:
ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते |
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ||१३-१३||
13.13.transliteration:
jñeyaṃ yattatpravakṣyāmi yajjñātvāmṛtamaśnute .
anādi matparaṃ brahma na sattannāsaducyate ||13-13||

translation.text: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere in the universe,—That exists pervading all.

lecture.title: 154. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 13-14 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.14.slok:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् |
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ||१३-१४||
13.14.transliteration:
sarvataḥ pāṇipādaṃ tatsarvato.akṣiśiromukham .
sarvataḥ śrutimalloke sarvamāvṛtya tiṣṭhati ||13-14||

translation.text: Shining by the functions of all the senses, yet without the senses; Absolute, yet sustaining all; devoid of Gunas, yet their experiencer.

lecture.title: 154. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 13-14 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.15.slok:
सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् |
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ||१३-१५||
13.15.transliteration:
sarvendriyaguṇābhāsaṃ sarvendriyavivarjitam .
asaktaṃ sarvabhṛccaiva nirguṇaṃ guṇabhoktṛ ca ||13-15||

translation.text: Without and within (all) beings; the unmoving and also the moving; because of Its subtlety incomprehensible; It is far and near.

lecture.title: 155. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verse 15 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.16.slok:
बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च |
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ||१३-१६||
13.16.transliteration:
bahirantaśca bhūtānāmacaraṃ carameva ca .
sūkṣmatvāttadavijñeyaṃ dūrasthaṃ cāntike ca tat ||13-16||

translation.text: Impartible, yet It exists as if divided in beings: It is to be known as sustaining beings; and devouring, as well as generating (them).

lecture.title: 156. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 16-18 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.17.slok:
अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् |
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ||१३-१७||
13.17.transliteration:
avibhaktaṃ ca bhūteṣu vibhaktamiva ca sthitam .
bhūtabhartṛ ca tajjñeyaṃ grasiṣṇu prabhaviṣṇu ca ||13-17||

translation.text: The Light even of lights, It is said to be beyond darkness; Knowledge, and the One Thing to be known, the Goal of' knowledge, dwelling in the hearts of all.

lecture.title: 157. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verse 17 Cont'd I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.18.slok:
ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते |
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ||१३-१८||
13.18.transliteration:
jyotiṣāmapi tajjyotistamasaḥ paramucyate .
jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ jñānagamyaṃ hṛdi sarvasya viṣṭhitam ||13-18||

translation.text: Thus Kshetra, knowledge, and that which has to be known, have been briefly stated. Knowing this, My devotee is fitted for My state.

lecture.title: 156. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 16-18 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.19.slok:
इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः |
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ||१३-१९||
13.19.transliteration:
iti kṣetraṃ tathā jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ coktaṃ samāsataḥ .
madbhakta etadvijñāya madbhāvāyopapadyate ||13-19||

translation.text: Know thou that Prakriti and Purusha are both beginningless; and know thou also that all modifications and Gunas are born of Prakriti.

lecture.title: 158. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 19-21 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.20.slok:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि |
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ||१३-२०||
13.20.transliteration:
prakṛtiṃ puruṣaṃ caiva viddhyanādi ubhāvapi .
vikārāṃśca guṇāṃścaiva viddhi prakṛtisambhavān ||13-20||

translation.text: In the production of the body and the senses, Prakriti is said to be the cause; in the experience of pleasure and pain, Purusha is said to be the cause.

lecture.title: 158. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 19-21 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.21.slok:
कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते |
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ||१३-२१||
13.21.transliteration:
kāryakāraṇakartṛtve hetuḥ prakṛtirucyate .
puruṣaḥ sukhaduḥkhānāṃ bhoktṛtve heturucyate ||13-21||

translation.text: Purusha seated in Prakriti, experiences the Gunas born of Prakriti; the reason of his birth in good and evil wombs is his attachment to the Gunas.

lecture.title: 158. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 19-21 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.22.slok:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् |
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ||१३-२२||
13.22.transliteration:
puruṣaḥ prakṛtistho hi bhuṅkte prakṛtijānguṇān .
kāraṇaṃ guṇasaṅgo.asya sadasadyonijanmasu ||13-22||

translation.text: And the Supreme Purusha in this body is also called the Looker-on, the Permitter, the Supporter, the Experiencer, the Great Lord, and the Highest Self.

lecture.title: 159. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 22-23 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.23.slok:
उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः |
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ||१३-२३||
13.23.transliteration:
upadraṣṭānumantā ca bhartā bhoktā maheśvaraḥ .
paramātmeti cāpyukto dehe.asminpuruṣaḥ paraḥ ||13-23||

translation.text: He who thus knows the Purusha and Prakriti together with the Gunas, whatever his life, is not born again.

lecture.title: 159. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 22-23 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.24.slok:
य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह |
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ||१३-२४||
13.24.transliteration:
ya evaṃ vetti puruṣaṃ prakṛtiṃ ca guṇaiḥ saha .
sarvathā vartamāno.api na sa bhūyo.abhijāyate ||13-24||

translation.text: Some by meditation behold the Self in their own intelligence by the purified heart, others by the path of knowledge, others again by Karma Yoga.

lecture.title: 160. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 24-25 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.25.slok:
ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना |
अन्ये साङ्ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ||१३-२५||
13.25.transliteration:
dhyānenātmani paśyanti kecidātmānamātmanā .
anye sāṅkhyena yogena karmayogena cāpare ||13-25||

translation.text: Others again not knowing thus, worship as they have heard from others. Even these go beyond death, regarding what they have heard as the Supreme Refuge.

lecture.title: 160. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 24-25 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.26.slok:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते |
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ||१३-२६||
13.26.transliteration:
anye tvevamajānantaḥ śrutvānyebhya upāsate .
te.api cātitarantyeva mṛtyuṃ śrutiparāyaṇāḥ ||13-26||

translation.text: Whatever being is born, the moving or the unmoving, O bull of the Bhâratas, know it to be from the union of Kshetra and Kshetrajna.

lecture.title: 161. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 26-28 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: He sees, who sees the Lord Supreme, existing equally in all beings, deathless in the dying.

lecture.title: 161. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 26-28 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.28.slok:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् |
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ||१३-२८||
13.28.transliteration:
samaṃ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṃ parameśvaram .
vinaśyatsvavinaśyantaṃ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||13-28||

translation.text: Since seeing the Lord equally existent everywhere, he injures not Self by self, and so goes to the highest Goal.

lecture.title: 161. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 26-28 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.29.slok:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् |
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ||१३-२९||
13.29.transliteration:
samaṃ paśyanhi sarvatra samavasthitamīśvaram .
na hinastyātmanātmānaṃ tato yāti parāṃ gatim ||13-29||

translation.text: He sees, who sees that all actions are done by Prakriti alone and that the Self is actionless.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.30.slok:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः |
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ||१३-३०||
13.30.transliteration:
prakṛtyaiva ca karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśaḥ .
yaḥ paśyati tathātmānamakartāraṃ sa paśyati ||13-30||

translation.text: When he sees the separate existence of all beings inherent in the One, and their expansion from That (One) alone, he then becomes Brahman.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.31.slok:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति |
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ||१३-३१||
13.31.transliteration:
yadā bhūtapṛthagbhāvamekasthamanupaśyati .
tata eva ca vistāraṃ brahma sampadyate tadā ||13-31||

translation.text: Being without beginning and devoid of Gunas, this Supreme Self, immutable, O son of Kunti, though existing in the body neither acts nor is affected.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.32.slok:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः |
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ||१३-३२||
13.32.transliteration:
anāditvānnirguṇatvātparamātmāyamavyayaḥ .
śarīrastho.api kaunteya na karoti na lipyate ||13-32||

translation.text: As the all-pervading Akâsha, because of its subtlety, is not tainted, so the Self existent in the body everywhere is not tainted.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.33.slok:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते |
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ||१३-३३||
13.33.transliteration:
yathā sarvagataṃ saukṣmyādākāśaṃ nopalipyate .
sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate ||13-33||

translation.text: As the one sun illumines all this world, so does He who abides in the Kshetra, O descendant of Bharata, illumine the whole Kshetra.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

13.34.slok:
यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः |
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ||१३-३४||
13.34.transliteration:
yathā prakāśayatyekaḥ kṛtsnaṃ lokamimaṃ raviḥ .
kṣetraṃ kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṃ prakāśayati bhārata ||13-34||

translation.text: They who thus with the eye of knowledge perceive the distinction between the Kshetra and the Kshetrajna, and also the emancipation from the Prakriti of beings, they go to the Supreme.

lecture.title: 162. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 13, Verses 29-34 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.1.slok:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् |
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ||१४-१||
14.1.transliteration:
śrībhagavānuvāca .
paraṃ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṃ jñānamuttamam .
yajjñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṃ siddhimito gatāḥ ||14-1||

translation.text: Again shall I tell thee that supreme knowledge which is above all knowledge, having known which all the Munis have attained to high perfection after this life.
translation.speaker: The Blessed Lord said:

lecture.title: 163. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 14, Verses 1-5  I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.2.slok:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः |
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ||१४-२||
14.2.transliteration:
idaṃ jñānamupāśritya mama sādharmyamāgatāḥ .
sarge.api nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca ||14-2||

translation.text: They who having devoted themselves to this knowledge, have attained to My Being, are neither born at the time of creation, nor are they troubled at the time of dissolution.

lecture.title: 163. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 14, Verses 1-5  I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.3.slok:
मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् |
सम्भवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ||१४-३||
14.3.transliteration:
mama yonirmahad brahma tasmingarbhaṃ dadhāmyaham .
sambhavaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ tato bhavati bhārata ||14-3||

translation.text: My womb is the great Prakriti; in that I place the germ; from thence, O descendant of Bharata, is the birth of all beings.

lecture.title: 163. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 14, Verses 1-5  I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.4.slok:
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः |
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ||१४-४||
14.4.transliteration:
sarvayoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ .
tāsāṃ brahma mahadyonirahaṃ bījapradaḥ pitā ||14-4||

translation.text: Whatever forms are produced, O son of Kunti, in all the wombs, the great Prakriti is their womb, and I the seed-giving Father.

lecture.title: 163. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 14, Verses 1-5  I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.5.slok:
सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः |
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ||१४-५||
14.5.transliteration:
sattvaṃ rajastama iti guṇāḥ prakṛtisambhavāḥ .
nibadhnanti mahābāho dehe dehinamavyayam ||14-5||

translation.text: Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas,—these Gunas, O mighty-armed, born of Prakriti, bind fast in the body the indestructible embodied one.

lecture.title: 163. Bhagavad Gita  | Chapter 14, Verses 1-5  I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.6.slok:
तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् |
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ||१४-६||
14.6.transliteration:
tatra sattvaṃ nirmalatvātprakāśakamanāmayam .
sukhasaṅgena badhnāti jñānasaṅgena cānagha ||14-6||

translation.text: Of these Sattva, from its stainlessness luminous and free from evil, binds, O sinless one, by attachment to happiness, and by attachment to knowledge.

lecture.title: 164. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verse 6 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.7.slok:
रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् |
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ||१४-७||
14.7.transliteration:
rajo rāgātmakaṃ viddhi tṛṣṇāsaṅgasamudbhavam .
tannibadhnāti kaunteya karmasaṅgena dehinam ||14-7||

translation.text: Know Rajas to be of the nature of passion, giving rise to thirst and attachment; it binds fast, O son of Kunti, the embodied one, by attachment to action.

lecture.title: 165. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 6-10 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.8.slok:
तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् |
प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ||१४-८||
14.8.transliteration:
tamastvajñānajaṃ viddhi mohanaṃ sarvadehinām .
pramādālasyanidrābhistannibadhnāti bhārata ||14-8||

translation.text: And know Tamas to be born of ignorance, stupefying all embodied beings; it binds fast, O descendant of Bharata, by miscomprehension, indolence, and sleep.

lecture.title: 165. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 6-10 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.9.slok:
सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत |
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ||१४-९||
14.9.transliteration:
sattvaṃ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata .
jñānamāvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayatyuta ||14-9||

translation.text: Sattva attaches to happiness, and Rajas to action, O descendant of Bharata; while Tamas, verily, shrouding discrimination, attaches to miscomprehension.

lecture.title: 165. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 6-10 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.10.slok:
रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत |
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ||१४-१०||
14.10.transliteration:
rajastamaścābhibhūya sattvaṃ bhavati bhārata .
rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamaścaiva tamaḥ sattvaṃ rajastathā ||14-10||

translation.text: Sattva arises, O descendant of Bharata, predominating over Rajas and 'Tamas; and Rajas over Sattva and Tamas; so, Tamas over Sattva and Rajas.

lecture.title: 165. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 6-10 I Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.11.slok:
सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते |
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ||१४-११||
14.11.transliteration:
sarvadvāreṣu dehe.asminprakāśa upajāyate .
jñānaṃ yadā tadā vidyādvivṛddhaṃ sattvamityuta ||14-11||

translation.text: When through every sense in this body, the light of intelligence shines, then it should be known that Sattva is predominant.

lecture.title: 166. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 11-15 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.12.slok:
लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा |
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ||१४-१२||
14.12.transliteration:
lobhaḥ pravṛttirārambhaḥ karmaṇāmaśamaḥ spṛhā .
rajasyetāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha ||14-12||

translation.text: Greed, activity, the undertaking of actions, unrest, longing—these arise when Rajas is predominant, O bull of the: Bhâratas.

lecture.title: 166. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 11-15 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.13.slok:
अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च |
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ||१४-१३||
14.13.transliteration:
aprakāśo.apravṛttiśca pramādo moha eva ca .
tamasyetāni jāyante vivṛddhe kurunandana ||14-13||

translation.text: Darkness, inertness, miscomprehension, and delusion,—these arise when Tamas is predominant, O descendant of Kuru.

lecture.title: 166. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 11-15 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.14.slok:
यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् |
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ||१४-१४||
14.14.transliteration:
yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṃ yāti dehabhṛt .
tadottamavidāṃ lokānamalānpratipadyate ||14-14||

translation.text: If the embodied one meets death when Sattva is predominant, then he attains to the spotless regions of the worshippers of the Highest.

lecture.title: 166. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 11-15 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.15.slok:
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते |
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ||१४-१५||
14.15.transliteration:
rajasi pralayaṃ gatvā karmasaṅgiṣu jāyate .
tathā pralīnastamasi mūḍhayoniṣu jāyate ||14-15||

translation.text: Meeting death in Rajas he is born among those attached to action; so dying in Tamas, he is born in the wombs of the irrational.

lecture.title: 166. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 11-15 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.16.slok:
कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् |
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ||१४-१६||
14.16.transliteration:
karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṃ nirmalaṃ phalam .
rajasastu phalaṃ duḥkhamajñānaṃ tamasaḥ phalam ||14-16||

translation.text: The fruit of good action, they say, is Sâttvika and pure; verily, the fruit of Rajas is pain, and ignorance is the fruit of Tamas.

lecture.title: 167. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 16-20 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.17.slok:
सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च |
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ||१४-१७||
14.17.transliteration:
sattvātsañjāyate jñānaṃ rajaso lobha eva ca .
pramādamohau tamaso bhavato.ajñānameva ca ||14-17||

translation.text: From Sattva arises wisdom, and greed from Rajas; miscomprehension, delusion and ignorance arise from Tamas.

lecture.title: 167. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 16-20 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.18.slok:
ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः |
जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ||१४-१८||
14.18.transliteration:
ūrdhvaṃ gacchanti sattvasthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ .
jaghanyaguṇavṛttisthā adho gacchanti tāmasāḥ ||14-18||

translation.text: The Sattva-abiding go upwards; the Râjasika dwell in the middle; and the Tâmasika, abiding in the function. of the lowest Guna, go downwards.

lecture.title: 167. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 16-20 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.19.slok:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति |
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ||१४-१९||
14.19.transliteration:
nānyaṃ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṃ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati .
guṇebhyaśca paraṃ vetti madbhāvaṃ so.adhigacchati ||14-19||

translation.text: When the seer beholds no agent other than the Gunas and knows That which is higher than the Gunas, he attains to My being.

lecture.title: 167. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 16-20 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: The embodied one having gone beyond these three Gunas out of which the body is evolved, is freed from birth, death, decay and pain, and attains to immortality.

lecture.title: 167. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 16-20 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.21.slok:
अर्जुन उवाच |
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो |
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ||१४-२१||
14.21.transliteration:
arjuna uvāca .
kairliṅgaistrīnguṇānetānatīto bhavati prabho .
kimācāraḥ kathaṃ caitāṃstrīnguṇānativartate ||14-21||

translation.text: By what marks, O Lord, is he (known) who has gone beyond these three Gunas? What is his conduct, and how does he pass beyond these three Gunas?
translation.speaker: Arjuna said:

lecture.title: 168. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 21-22 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.22.slok:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव |
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ||१४-२२||
14.22.transliteration:
śrībhagavānuvāca .
prakāśaṃ ca pravṛttiṃ ca mohameva ca pāṇḍava .
na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati ||14-22||

translation.text: He who hates not the appearance of light, (the effect of Sattva), activity (the effect of Rajas), and delusion (the effect of Tamas), (in his own mind), O Pândava, nor longs for them when absent;
translation.speaker: The Blessed Lord said:

lecture.title: 168. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 21-22 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.23.slok:
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते |
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ||१४-२३||
14.23.transliteration:
udāsīnavadāsīno guṇairyo na vicālyate .
guṇā vartanta ityevaṃ yo.avatiṣṭhati neṅgate ||14-23||

translation.text: He who, sitting like one unconcerned, is moved not by the Gunas, who, knowing that the Gunas operate, is Self-centred and swerves not;

lecture.title: 169. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 23-25 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

translation.text: Alike in pleasure and pain, Self-abiding, regarding a clod of earth, a stone and gold alike; the same to agreeable and disagreeable, firm, the same in censure and, praise;

lecture.title: 169. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 23-25 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.25.slok:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः |
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ||१४-२५||
14.25.transliteration:
mānāpamānayostulyastulyo mitrāripakṣayoḥ .
sarvārambhaparityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa ucyate ||14-25||

translation.text: The same in honour and disgrace, the same to friend and foe, relinquishing all undertakings—he is said to have gone beyond the Gunas.

lecture.title: 169. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 23-25 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.26.slok:
मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते |
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ||१४-२६||
14.26.transliteration:
māṃ ca yo.avyabhicāreṇa bhaktiyogena sevate .
sa guṇānsamatītyaitānbrahmabhūyāya kalpate ||14-26||

translation.text: And he who serves Me with an unswerving devotion, he, going beyond the Gunas, is fitted for becoming Brahman.

lecture.title: 170. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 26-27 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

14.27.slok:
ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च |
शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ||१४-२७||
14.27.transliteration:
brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāhamamṛtasyāvyayasya ca .
śāśvatasya ca dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya ca ||14-27||

translation.text: For I am the abode of Brahman, the Immortal and Immutable, of everlasting Dharma and of Absolute Bliss.

lecture.title: 170. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 14, Verses 26-27 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.1.slok:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् |
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ||१५-१||
15.1.transliteration:
śrībhagavānuvāca .
ūrdhvamūlamadhaḥśākhamaśvatthaṃ prāhuravyayam .
chandāṃsi yasya parṇāni yastaṃ veda sa vedavit ||15-1||

translation.text: They speak of an eternal Ashvattha rooted above and branching below, whose leaves are the Vedas; he who knows it, is a Veda-knower.
translation.speaker: The Blessed Lord said:

lecture.title: 174. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 1-4 (Contd.)  | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.2.slok:
अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः |
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि
कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ||१५-२||
15.2.transliteration:
adhaścordhvaṃ prasṛtāstasya śākhā guṇapravṛddhā viṣayapravālāḥ .
adhaśca mūlānyanusantatāni karmānubandhīni manuṣyaloke ||15-2||

translation.text: Below and above spread its branches, nourished by the Gunas; sense-objects are its buds; and below in the world of man stretch forth the roots, originating action.

lecture.title: 174. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 1-4 (Contd.)  | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.3.slok:
न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा |
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलं
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ||१५-३||
15.3.transliteration:
na rūpamasyeha tathopalabhyate nānto na cādirna ca sampratiṣṭhā .
aśvatthamenaṃ suvirūḍhamūlaṃ asaṅgaśastreṇa dṛḍhena chittvā ||15-3||

translation.text: Its form is not here perceived as such, neither its end, nor its origin, nor its existence. Having cut asunder this firm-rooted Ashvattha with the strong axe of non-attachment,—then that Goal is to be sought for, going whither they (the wise) do not return again. I seek refuge in that Primeval Purusha whence streamed forth the Eternal Activity.

lecture.title: 174. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 1-4 (Contd.)  | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.4.slok:
ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं
यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः |
तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये |
यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ||१५-४||
15.4.transliteration:
tataḥ padaṃ tatparimārgitavyaṃ yasmingatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ .
tameva cādyaṃ puruṣaṃ prapadye .
yataḥ pravṛttiḥ prasṛtā purāṇī ||15-4||

translation.text: Its form is not here perceived as such, neither its end, nor its origin, nor its existence. Having cut asunder this firm-rooted Ashvattha with the strong axe of non-attachment,—then that Goal is to be sought for, going whither they (the wise) do not return again. I seek refuge in that Primeval Purusha whence streamed forth the Eternal Activity.

lecture.title: 174. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 1-4 (Contd.)  | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.5.slok:
निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः |
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञैर्-
गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ||१५-५||
15.5.transliteration:
nirmānamohā jitasaṅgadoṣā adhyātmanityā vinivṛttakāmāḥ .
dvandvairvimuktāḥ sukhaduḥkhasaṃjñaira- gacchantyamūḍhāḥ padamavyayaṃ tat ||15-5||

translation.text: Free from pride and delusion, with the evil of attachment conquered, ever dwelling in the Self, with desires completely receded, liberated from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, the undeluded reach that Goal Eternal.

lecture.title: 175. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verse 5 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.6.slok:
न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः |
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ||१५-६||
15.6.transliteration:
na tadbhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ .
yadgatvā na nivartante taddhāma paramaṃ mama ||15-6||

translation.text: That the sun illumines not, nor the moon, nor fire; that is My Supreme Abode, going whither they return not.

lecture.title: 176. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verse 6 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.7.slok:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः |
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ||१५-७||
15.7.transliteration:
mamaivāṃśo jīvaloke jīvabhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ .
manaḥṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛtisthāni karṣati ||15-7||

translation.text: An eternal portion of Myself having become a living soul in the world of life, draws (to itself) the (five) senses with mind for the sixth, abiding in Prakriti.

lecture.title: 177. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 7-9 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.8.slok:
शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः |
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ||१५-८||
15.8.transliteration:
śarīraṃ yadavāpnoti yaccāpyutkrāmatīśvaraḥ .
gṛhitvaitāni saṃyāti vāyurgandhānivāśayāt ||15-8||

translation.text: When the Lord obtains a body and when He leaves it, He takes these and goes, as the wind takes the scents from their seats (the flowers).

lecture.title: 177. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 7-9 | Swami Sarvapriyananda

15.9.slok:
श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च |
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ||१५-९||
15.9.transliteration:
śrotraṃ cakṣuḥ sparśanaṃ ca rasanaṃ ghrāṇameva ca .
adhiṣṭhāya manaścāyaṃ viṣayānupasevate ||15-9||

translation.text: Presiding over the ear, the eye, the touch, the taste and the smell, as also the mind, He experiences objects.

lecture.title: 177. Bhagavad Gita | Chapter 15, Verses 7-9 | Swami Sarvapriyananda
